messages.content: Set into tv show The Arrow universe. Sarah lance and Nysa Al ghul are in a relationship. Both find themselves interested in new member Al Mansi (previously known as Nicolas before joining the ranks). He is a shy, young man. His dedication to training is second to none and he is making progress faster than anyone before him. He is also Someone who should easily be forgotten. His unassuming nature make him someone nobody really would think as an assassin. Story start with Al Mansi in the training halls. He as progressed by leaps and bound and is on part with both Sarab and Nyssa in hand to hand combat with a style leaning more on hard style. He is now Working on his meteor hammer. Both Nyssa and Sarah propose to help him. Nyssa proposes to start training in long range weapons like the bow and arrow. Training session. Written like fiction book.No declaration of romantic interest between Nyssa, Sarah & Al Mansi.

Al Mansi stood in the training hall, his meteor hammer held tightly in his hand. He had come a long way since joining the League of Assassins, and he knew that his progress had not gone unnoticed. He had caught the eye of both Nyssa Al Ghul and Sarah Lance, two of the most skilled fighters in the League. 

As he began to practice with his meteor hammer, he could see the two women watching him intently. He knew that they were observing his technique and analyzing his every move. But Al Mansi was determined to impress them, to show them that he was worthy of their attention.

Despite his shyness, Al Mansi was immensely dedicated to his training. He pushed himself harder than anyone else, always striving to improve. And it was paying off. He had already surpassed many of the other recruits, and he was quickly catching up to the likes of Nyssa and Sarah.

Nyssa approached him, a bow and quiver of arrows in her hands. "You've been doing well with your close combat training," she said, "but I think it's time to expand your skillset. Would you be interested in learning long-range weapons?"

Al Mansi nodded eagerly. He had always been keen to learn new techniques, and he knew that Nyssa was one of the best archers in the League. He couldn't pass up the opportunity to train with her.

The three of them began their session, with Nyssa teaching Al Mansi the basics of archery. He struggled at first, as his focus had always been on close combat. But soon he began to grasp the fundamentals, and his aim steadily improved.

Meanwhile, Sarah took Al Mansi through a series of drills with his meteor hammer. She was impressed by his natural talent with the weapon, but she could see that he still had a long way to go. She pushed him harder, urging him to unleash his full potential.

The training session lasted for hours, but Al Mansi soaked up every bit of knowledge that Nyssa and Sarah had to offer. He knew that he still had a lot to learn, but he was more determined than ever to become the best assassin he could be.

As the session came to a close, Nyssa and Sarah both praised Al Mansi for his hard work and dedication. And though they didn't show it, they both knew that there was something special about this young man. He may have been unassuming, but he had the potential to be one of the greatest assassins the League had ever seen. It was a potential that they would continue to foster, as they worked together to bring out the best in Al Mansi.

Month passed and they became a team. As time passes, their team became the stuff of legends even by league standards. Gaining the notice of Ras AlGhul who, with his more than keen eyes, realized that the three were dancing around each other. Yet their combined skills couldn’t be ignored. So he remained silent on the matter. As long as those three knews that their loyalty to the league was most important, he would allow it. No love declaration between Nyssa, Sarah & Al Mansi.

As their training continued, the bond between Nyssa, Sarah, and Al Mansi grew stronger. They became a formidable team, their skills complementing each other's in ways that were nothing short of awe-inspiring. They quickly gained a reputation as the best assassins in the League, and their exploits were whispered about in hushed tones even amongst their fellow members.

Ras Al Ghul watched their progress with interest, noting the way the three worked together seamlessly and effortlessly. He saw the way they shared glances and knew that they were more than just teammates. But he also knew that they were loyal to the League and would never let their personal feelings interfere with their duties.

So he remained silent, content to watch as the trio continued to impress everyone around them. They completed mission after mission, their success rate unmatched by anyone else in the League.

Despite the unspoken attraction between them, the three of them remained focused on their training, constantly seeking ways to improve their skills and hone their techniques. They knew that the life of an assassin was a dangerous one, and they were determined to be ready for anything that came their way.

With each passing day, their bond grew stronger, their trust in each other unshakable. And while they never declared their love for each other, they didn't need to. Their actions spoke louder than words, and their loyalty to each other and to the League was all that mattered.

As years passed, Al Mansi presence helped Sarah not lost herself in the killing. And when news of the undertakings, of the Glades of Starling City reach them, he offered support. « Maybe I could go there in your place. Make sure your loved one there are self and learn how it was possible that someone such as Al Saher could betray us without anyone noticing. It could be useful in the futur too. Nysa what do you think your father will say about it ? ». In the end, Ras Al Ghul agreed for Al Mansi to go. But without Sarah nor Nyssa. Sarah was too much emotionally involved & Nyssa needed to be there for the time being. Al Mansi mission was to learn how Malcom Merlyn could have done that without being notice by the league, who was the warrior who had managed to defeat him & learn more of this man. On a more personal note, he would make sure that Sarah family was still there.

Al Mansi arrived in Starling City, blending in with the crowd as he went about his reconnaissance. He kept a low profile, careful not to draw attention to himself as he delved deeper into the city's underworld.

It didn't take long for him to learn about Malcolm Merlyn and his role in the events that had taken place in the Glades. Al Mansi was astounded by how the League had failed to notice Merlyn's treachery, and he made a mental note to report back to Ras Al Ghul with his findings.

As he dug deeper, Al Mansi discovered that the warrior who had defeated Merlyn was none other than the Green Arrow, a vigilante who had quickly become a legend in Starling City. Al Mansi was intrigued by the man behind the mask and made it his mission to learn as much as he could about him.

But Al Mansi's primary concern was with Sarah's family. He made sure to check in on them regularly, discreetly watching over them from afar to ensure their safety. He was relieved to see that they were doing well, despite the turmoil that had been unleashed upon the city.

As his mission drew to a close, Al Mansi reflected on the lessons he had learned during his time in Starling City. He had gained valuable insights into the inner workings of the League and had come to appreciate the importance of vigilance and attention to detail.

Back in the League, he reported his findings to Ras Al Ghul, who was impressed with Al Mansi's dedication and resolve. The young assassin had proven himself to be a valuable asset to the League, and Ras Al Ghul knew that he would continue to be a force to be reckoned with in the years to come.

With both Nyssa & Sarah presents, Al Mansi made his report on the situation. His belief that there was more infolding in the city, that something dangerous was making its way to Starling City. He also reported his findings about Oliver Queen, his clumsy (by league standards) skills & how he believed that either Oliver Queen had been lucky or ready to die to defeat his opponent. If his insctincts were rights, Oliver Queen, The Arrow, may not be in a position to protect his city from what lay ahead.

Nyssa and Sarah listened intently to Al Mansi's report, impressed by the thoroughness of his investigation. They shared his concern about the danger that was lurking in Starling City, and they knew that they needed to take action to protect the League's interests.

As Al Mansi spoke about the Green Arrow, Nyssa and Sarah exchanged glances. They knew that they needed to find a way to work with him, to leverage his skills and resources to help them in their mission.

"The Green Arrow may be our only hope in this situation," Nyssa said, breaking the silence. "If we can convince him to work with us, we may be able to stave off the danger that is coming."

Sarah nodded in agreement. "But we need to be careful," she warned. "We don't know who we can trust in Starling City, and the League's interests may not align with those of the Green Arrow."

With a plan in place, the three of them set out to find the Green Arrow. They knew that time was of the essence, and that they needed to act quickly if they were to avoid a catastrophe in Starling City.

With Nyssa & Sarah gathering information, it was Al Mansi who intervene to save The Hood from a potential lethal injection from the centrifuge thief. The staredown between the two, with Team Arrow trying to learn more, would become legendary.

As Al Mansi intervened to save the Green Arrow from certain death, tension filled the air. The two assassins locked eyes, taking each other measure. The rest of Team Arrow watched the exchange, unsure of what was happening.

Slowly, Al Mansi stepped back, holstering his weapon. "I was sent here to help," he said, his tone even. "I believe that our interests may align with yours."

The Green Arrow eyed him warily. "And what interests would those be?"

Al Mansi looked over at Nyssa and Sarah, who had approached to join them. "The security of Starling City. We believe that there is a significant threat on the horizon, and we need your help to stop it."

The Green Arrow considered his words carefully. "And what makes you so sure that I can trust you?"

Nyssa stepped forward, her voice firm. "We are members of the League of Assassins. We seek to protect our interests and prevent harm to innocent lives. We know that our methods may not align with yours, but we believe that we can work together to achieve our goals."

The Green Arrow studied the three assassins carefully, weighing his options. He knew that he couldn't handle the threat on his own, and he needed all the help he could get.

"Okay," he said finally. "But let's make one thing clear. I'm in charge here. And if anything goes wrong, it's on you."

The three assassins nodded in agreement, and the partnership was sealed. With the Green Arrow's help, they would face the danger that was lurking in Starling City, and work together to protect innocent lives and preserve the balance of power. The staredown between Al Mansi and the Green Arrow would go down in history as the moment that two of the most skilled fighters in the world joined forces to take on a common enemy.

"Lets make one more thing clear, mister Queen. We've come first here you couldn't stop Malcom Merlyn plan to unleashed totally." The powerplay between The Hood & Al Mansi would lead to a lot of questions later. "And before you ask, you're just not as skilled in subterfuge & secrecy as you should being"

The Hood bristled at Al Mansi's words, his hands clenching into fists. But he knew that the assassin had a point. The events in the Glades had been catastrophic, and he had failed to prevent the destruction that had been unleashed upon the city.

"Fine," he said through gritted teeth. "But we need to move quickly. What's the plan?"

Al Mansi outlined the situation, explaining what he and his team had discovered about the looming danger in Starling City. The Hood listened carefully, impressed by the thoroughness of their investigation.

Together, they plotted their next moves, working out a strategy that would help them to stay one step ahead of their enemy. It was a tense few days, as they raced against time to prevent disaster from striking. But with the combined skills of the three assassins and the Green Arrow, they were able to thwart the danger and save countless lives.

When it was all over, the partnership between the League of Assassins and the Green Arrow was the talk of the town. People wondered how such disparate forces could have succeeded in working together, and many questioned the motives of the assassins involved.

But for Al Mansi, Nyssa, and Sarah, the success of the mission was all that mattered. They had saved lives and preserved the balance of power. And maybe, just maybe, they had gained a newfound respect for the Green Arrow in the process.

As their mission drew to a close, Ras Al Ghul called back Nyssa & Sarah, placing Starling City under Al Mansi juridiction until his man was sure that Oliver Queen would not become a threat to the League or to the Balance between Evil & Justice. Al Mansi smile as his two bestfriends, the two women he had fallen in love with. He watched them leave, still keeping silent about his own feeling. The time, he believed, was not right for such a declaration.

Al Mansi watched as Nyssa and Sarah left Starling City, feeling a pang of sadness at their departure. He had grown to respect and admire the two women, and he knew that he would miss their company.

But he also knew that he had a job to do. With Starling City under his jurisdiction, he worked tirelessly to ensure that the balance between evil and justice was preserved. He kept a watchful eye on Oliver Queen, monitoring his movements and behavior for any signs of trouble.

As the months wore on, Al Mansi became more and more entrenched in his role as the guardian of Starling City. He worked closely with the Green Arrow, and the two became an unstoppable force in the fight against crime and corruption.

But despite his successes, Al Mansi couldn't shake the feeling that something was missing. He thought about Nyssa and Sarah often, wondering what they were up to and hoping that they were safe.

And deep down, he knew that his feelings for the two women had only grown stronger over time. But he also knew that the time was not right for a declaration of love. He had a duty to the League and to Starling City, and he couldn't afford to let personal feelings cloud his judgment.

So he continued to work tirelessly, keeping the city safe and preserving the balance of power. And he kept his feelings to himself, waiting for the day when the time would be right for him to finally speak his heart.
"Who were your partners ?" Oliver Queen asked, one night. "The Heir To the Demon & Ta-er al-Sahfer" was Al Mansi answer. He was not going to developp more.

Oliver Queen raised an eyebrow, his curiosity piqued by Al Mansi's response. He had heard of both Nyssa and Ta-er al-Sahfer, and knew them to be ruthless assassins. He wondered what had brought them to Starling City, and why they had worked with Al Mansi.

But he could see the reluctance in Al Mansi's eyes, and he knew that the assassin wasn't going to divulge any more information. So he let the matter drop, knowing that there were some things that were best left unsaid.

Instead, they went back to discussing their mission, strategizing and planning their next moves. Despite their differences, the two men had developed a grudging respect for each other, borne out of their shared commitment to justice and the greater good.

And as they worked together to protect Starling City, Al Mansi couldn't help but wonder what the future held for him and his two friends. He knew that their paths were bound to cross again, and he hoped that when that day came, they would be able to work together once more.

It was Al Mansi who stop Roy Harper killing a cop in a mirakuru rage induce frenzy. After a fight, Al Mansi bring back the young man to the cave where he knew The Arrow would be. "Your protege need to learn to control himself. He nearly killed a cop. Next time... well... lets not ponder on such things..."

The Arrow nodded grimly at Al Mansi's words, his concern for Roy Harper evident in his eyes. He knew that the young man was still struggling to control his mirakuru-induced rage, and that it was only a matter of time before something like this happened.

"Thank you for bringing him back," he said, motioning for Al Mansi to set Roy down. "I'll take it from here."

Al Mansi nodded, stepping back as The Arrow approached Roy. He watched as the vigilante worked to calm Roy down, using all of his training and skills to bring the young man back from the brink of his rage.

It was a tense few minutes, as The Arrow talked Roy down and helped him to regain control of his emotions. But eventually, the danger passed, and Roy returned to his normal state.

The Arrow turned to Al Mansi, gratitude evident on his face. "Thank you for your help," he said. "I don't know what we would have done without you."

Al Mansi smiled, a small feeling of satisfaction swelling in his chest. He had helped to prevent a tragedy, and that was all that mattered.

As he left the cave, Al Mansi thought about Roy and the struggles he faced. He knew that the young man had a long road ahead of him, and that it would take a lot of hard work and determination to conquer his inner demons.

But he also knew that with the support of The Arrow and their team, anything was possible. And he felt a flicker of hope, knowing that together, they could overcome any obstacle that came their way.
Al Mansi also knew he need to prepare himself to face against potential mirakuru-enhanced opponents. Opponents that may very well may be in a better position to pose a threat.

As Al Mansi left the cave, his thoughts turned to the potential danger posed by mirakuru-enhanced opponents. He knew that they were a force to be reckoned with, possessing incredible strength and agility that could easily overpower a lesser opponent.

He also knew that he needed to be prepared to face them, to develop a strategy that would
